attacking_style: Targets early breakaways with methodical climb-by-climb point accumulation, securing position before main race broadcast begins. Prefers long-distance moves that survive through peloton disorganization rather than late explosive attacks.
team_tactics: Shows limited team coordination - breakaway autonomy emphasized over integrated squad support. Evidence of communication breakdowns with teammates attacking separately from peloton rather than supporting established breakaway positions.
terrain_preference: Rolling stages with multiple categorized climbs suited for KOM point accumulation. Benefits from technical finales where peloton chase cohesion breaks down (crashes, narrow roads). Not a pure climber or sprinter.
vulnerability: Relies on peloton disinterest or tactical errors to survive breakaways. When teams organize coordinated chases without incident, success rate appears compromised. Weak team backing limits ability to control races independently.
key_patterns: Early breakaway specialist, establishes moves before television coverage begins (2 observations)
Targets KOM points systematically across all categorized climbs in stage (2 observations)
Success dependent on peloton disruption: crashes, gaps, or chase coordination failures (2 observations)
Operates independently from team structure, limited domestique support evident (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2018 showed proactive winning form with KOM success, but 2020 reduced to defensive gap management with team coordination issues
